---
title: "2b: Mash Filtration & Clustering Report"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Overview

This report covers mash-distance-based genome filtration and hierarchical clustering.
Genomes are filtered by pairwise ANI (mash distance < 0.05 to reference strains),
then iteratively clustered until all clusters meet a minimum size threshold.

## Raw Mash Distances

![Raw mash distance heatmap](../output/figures/2b_mash_heatmap.png){width=80%}

## Distance Distribution

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![All mash distances](../output/figures/2b_mash_dist_all.png)

![Post-filtration](../output/figures/2b_mash_dist_filtered.png)
:::

## Sensitivity Analysis

![Clustering threshold sensitivity analysis](../output/figures/2b_sensitivity_analysis.png){width=60%}

## Clustering Results

### Initial Clustering

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Initial clustermap](../output/figures/2b_clustermap_initial.png)

![Initial cluster sizes](../output/figures/2b_cluster_sizes_initial.png)
:::

### Final Clustering (after small-cluster removal)

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Final clustermap](../output/figures/2b_clustermap_final.png)

![Final cluster sizes](../output/figures/2b_cluster_sizes_final.png)
:::

## Summary Statistics

```{python}
import pandas as pd

metadata = pd.read_csv("../temp/2b_genome_metadata.csv", dtype={"genome_id": str})
print(f"Final genomes: {metadata.shape[0]}")
print(f"Mash clusters: {metadata['mash_cluster'].nunique()}")
print()
print("Cluster sizes:")
print(metadata["mash_cluster"].value_counts().sort_index().to_string())
```
